solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    bool isPowerOfFour(int n) {
        return n > 0 && (n & (n - 1)) == 0 && (n & 0x55555555) != 0;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <This solution uses bitwise operations to check if n is a power of four. First, it checks if n is a power of two (n & (n - 1) == 0), and then checks if it is also a power of four by ensuring only certain bits are set (n & 0x55555555 != 0). This approach avoids loops and recursion, making it very efficient.>
